name: Death by Chocolate
recipeIngredient: 3 squares of unsweet chocolate, finely chopped (1 oz each)
2 squares of semisweet chocolate, finely chopped (1 oz each)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the first 3 ingredients in the top of a double boiler over simmering water. Stir occasionally until melted.
2. Remove chocolate mixture from heat and set aside.
3. In a large bowl, beat eggs, sugar, and vanilla with an electric mixer on medium-high speed until thickened (about 1 1/2 minutes).
4. Gradually add the chocolate mixture to the egg mixture, beating at medium speed until blended.
5. Sift flour, baking powder, salt into another bowl. Add this dry mixture to the chocolate mixture, stirring until combined.
6. Stir in sour cream. Pour batter into a greased and floured 9-inch round cake pan.
7. Bake at 325°F (165°C) for 40 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in center comes out clean. Let cool on a wire rack for 5 minutes, then remove from the pan and refrigerate uncovered for 20 minutes.
8. Remove from refrigerator. Cut brownie horizontally with a serrated knife; place top half cut side down into a 9-inch springform pan.
9. Pour 1 1/2 cups chocolate ganache over the layer.
10. Trim meringue to fit inside the pan, placing it upside down on the chocolate ganache.
11. Spread mocha mousse evenly over the meringue and top with remaining brownie layer cut side down. Refrigerate for 1 hour or place in freezer for 30 minutes.
12. Remove from refrigerator. Using a star-shaped decorating tip, pipe chocolate mousse stars on top of the cake. Cover and chill for 4 to 8 hours before serving.
13. To serve, heat a serrated knife under hot running water and dry with a towel. Arrange cake slice in center of plate and pour 2-4 tablespoons mocha rum sauce over the top. Serve immediately.
